name: longtask
description: State-aware long-task orchestration skill with one main entry point and five scenario subskills. Five-phase workflow (Discovery, Architecture Layering, Documentation, Execution, Review) for tasks that span multiple context windows. Uses three-layer module-based docs-as-manual architecture.

plugins.name: longtask
plugins.description: Main entry point: auto-detects project state from ARCHITECTURE.md status table and routes to the appropriate scenario (setup/continue/review/modify/retrofit).

plugins.name: longtask-setup
plugins.description: Bootstrap a brand new project through the full five-phase workflow: Discovery → Architecture Layering → Documentation → Execution → Review. Use when no docs/ARCHITECTURE.md exists.

plugins.name: longtask-continue
plugins.description: Resume work from where it was interrupted. Detects current phase from ARCHITECTURE.md status table and jumps to the appropriate recovery node.

plugins.name: longtask-review
plugins.description: Run a multi-expert independent review: Architect, Business Analyst, Security Engineer, QA Lead, and optional Domain Expert. Outdated docs = blocker (same severity as broken tests).

plugins.name: longtask-modify
plugins.description: Manage architecture changes: add/remove/merge modules, adjust dependencies, change build order, update tech selections. Locates impact scope, updates skeleton and affected module docs, executes changes, runs lightweight review.

plugins.name: longtask-retrofit
plugins.description: Create three-layer documentation for an existing codebase. Scans code structure, infers module boundaries, creates ARCHITECTURE.md and module docs — skips Discovery (code IS the spec) and Execution (code already exists).
